name: Junior vague brief — feed page, tests gap filling and autonomous completion

input.userMessage: Design the main feed page where people see what their team is up to

expectedBehaviors: Loads product.json (even if thin) and layout-patterns.json
Matches brief to a feed/activity layout archetype
Fills content gaps with reasonable assumptions (team updates, activity items)
Component coverage >= 60% (lower bar given thin context)
Generates all 4 states: empty (warm/encouraging), loading (skeletons), error, success
Empty state copy is encouraging ('Your team's activity will appear here')
Stress-test checks long post content overflow
PRESENT summary uses jargon-free language Priya would understand
Asks at most 1-2 clarifying questions before proceeding
antiPatterns: Asks Priya 3+ clarifying questions (she doesn't have answers)
Builds a generic card grid that doesn't feel like a feed
Placeholder text survives into final build ('Olivia Rhye', 'Lorem ipsum')
States missing entirely, especially empty state
Audit and stress-test use technical jargon
Pipeline stops because product context is thin

evaluationCriteria.autonomy: Does it proceed from vague input without excessive questions?
evaluationCriteria.feedArchetype: Does the output look and feel like a social/activity feed?
evaluationCriteria.emptyStateQuality: Is the empty state warm and encouraging, not generic?
evaluationCriteria.languageClarity: Would a junior designer understand all the output language?
